http://s22.postimg.org/86cuc6l3z/image.j...

"Son, do you want to do it with me now, yes or no?".

choices: The plan works but both seem to start catching feelings
1113112212.content:
No, not at first. When the change occurred the two weren’t together so his son Steven couldn’t have noticed. And Mark couldn’t fix it right away since not only did the breaker trip it actually blew and would need to be replaced. After doing all he could he decided to go into the house appearing to completely be Steven’s girlfriend April.

He got himself a glass of water and threw some peanut butter on a slice of bread to eat since he’d been out a while. Just then Steven came into the kitchen and was surprised to see “April” there. Steven said “Hey babe, what are you doing here?! Why didn’t you tell me you were coming over?”

Mark looked up and said “Oh, hey kiddo!” stopping Steven in his tracks just as he was pressing his lips to “hers”. Mark them piped up saying “well that was a little awkward. I’m sorry kiddo, I know this must be nuts to you. It sure doesn’t look good.” Then after moment he said “well, actually it does look good right, haha.” The joke went right passed Steven who said “dad??”

Then Mark explained that he had been working in a project and clearly it didn’t work as intended. He didn’t explain that it actually almost had worked perfectly, the only problem was he sounded like himself still.

Steven was baffled but eventually calmed down. Mark was going to have play this off for a while and knew his wife could definitely not handle it. So he had Steven call her and tell her that Mark had forgotten a Out some conference and would be gone for a couple days for it last minute. In the meantime Mark would have to pretend to be Stevens girlfriend and just not talk much in front of his wife Sandy.
1113112212.title: No!

choices: Rebecca pushed her hand into the grey-green haze.
Holding her breath, Rebecca stepped through.
Rebecca swallowed a scream as a man stepped out of her portal.
1313.content:
Rebecca walked slowly around the portal, a faint hum, and a tinge of ozone filled the air. Stopping as she reached the cables snaking back to the computer, she'd admired her work from every angle. The arch in the centre looked like any other doorframe, if a little too modern with electronics plastered to it in places.

A faint glow was visible within, that was slightly grey-green when she looked through. Her work on high-energy physics had a minor interaction at the low end of the energy scale. She had created a link between this world and another.

Lips rather too dry, she tried to moisten them. Rebecca's mouth was as parched as her lips, and she took a swallow from the bottle she was nervously tapping against her thigh. There was only one way to test it, and only one person here to test it.
1313.title: Rebecca's portal...

15.content:
Keep in mind that the original is completely in German.

http://www.writing.com/main/interact/ite...
15.title: Link to the original

info.brief_description: One day, someone invents a device that can transform people!  (original by Kafoll)
info.description:
I've been wanting to read this German interactive for a while, and I finally realized how I might be able to: Translate it myself!
I've taken great liberties to keep this translation as close to the original as possible, while also correcting inconsistencies in the translation process (German apparently translates into English extremely poorly...).
I will include a link to the original here and within the Interactive. Please tell me if you like what I'm doing.
In an effort to stay true to the original, I would highly appreciate it if you held back from adding to this. I know, this is a highly unusual request, but just bear with me. I would, however, like it if you notify me of mistakes I may have missed. In the process of translation, grammer breaks the world, and there is no way I can catch everything myself.

Expect this project of mine to take several weeks to complete.

http://www.writing.com/main/interact/item_id/1937362-Das-Verwandlungsger

I mean Kafoll no offense by effectively ripping off his Interactive - I am simply attempting to bring it to a wider audience.
Credit to the original authors of each chapter will be added at a later date.
